reviews.review_msg: She's an amazing prof. Helps all students as much as she can and is super caring. One of my favorite profs in SFU.
She has an attitude problem and is impatient with her students. She talks too fast, and is terribly disorganized,  MALORGANISé! Unless if you are a french pro, don't bother with her.
Overall, one of the best in the French department. Very lively classes, material was communicated clearly, grading was mostly fair. I really enjoyed Madame Blancard!
High expectations but lots of help available. Very receptive to comments, really tries to help each person understand as opposed to just blathering on. Watch grammar and anglicismes.

I've had her for three consecutive semesters now in 221, 222 and 300 and she is great! She is very kind and approachable and really enjoys making the class laugh. When it comes to marking there's not that much you can do as a French prof (either you got it wrong or you didn't). She is great!

She is a phenomenal professor who has truly mastered teaching. Despite FREN222 covering lots of content I found to be difficult, Paola managed to make these topics seem simple, logical, and approachable. Her constant feedback and many practice problems helped me succeed on exams and writes. Exams were fair and were similar to practice problems.
Paola is one of the professors I've ever had, I would take another class with her in a heart beat. She is wonderful.

She's probably the best prof in the French department. Puts together lectures really well, and is engaging, lots of socializing in class so u make lots of friends! But, she's a tough grader, and she expects you to know certain French rules that we wouldn't know already since French isn't our first language! Also, she can be sassy in her emails.

Paola is lovely - she's very kind, friendly, and easygoing. She makes sure that everyone understands and is always very helpful. Material was presented clearly. Tests and assignments were straightforward, and her expectations and feedback were always extremely clear and fair. Great prof! I would take her classes again.

One of the best French profs I had at the SFU French department. I learned and improved after being in her class. Her lessons are amazing, and are very straight-forward and easy to understand. She's very open to her students. I love her!
Linda is the best. Organized, helpful and approachable.
Wonderful professor.  Wish she would teach upper division classes, though.  I found her to be super supportive and kind.
Linda is an amazing teacher! She can understand any question you ask even when you have problems explaining it.  My only concern is that she only teachers lower divison classes.
